tiktok.caption: Your brain is basically drunk by 4 PM — and every retailer knows it. Here's the $1,400 science behind decision fatigue.

youtube_short.title: Decision Fatigue Is Draining $1,400/Year
youtube_short.description: Your brain makes 35,000 decisions a day — and by 4 PM it's running on empty. An Israeli judge study found the same judge approved parole 65% of the time in the morning but just 20% before lunch. Retailers have known this for decades — that's why sale emails hit your inbox at 4 and 5 PM. The result? Americans spend 22% more on late-day purchases, draining $1,400 a year from the average wallet. The fix: make every big money decision before 10 AM. #personalfinance #decisionfatigue #moneytips Follow for daily money psychology breakdowns.
